settings.title: Settings
settings.dnsHint: Applied to the runtime config. "Default" inherits profile/core values.

tun.title: TUN & traffic tuning
tun.hint: Layered on top of the active subscription — pick “Default” to inherit it. Applied live, no core restart.

settings.updatePlatformNote: Automatic install is Windows-only — open the release page to update on this platform.
settings.upToDate: You are on the latest release.

resetModal.title: Reset app settings
resetModal.keepProfilesBody: Reset UI settings and local defaults, but keep profiles?
resetModal.withProfilesBody: Reset settings and delete all profiles from this device?

tour.connectBody: Use this button to start or stop the embedded core. After you import a subscription, Connect turns traffic on according to the mode and traffic switches next to it.
tour.modeBody: Rule uses your rules list. Global sends most traffic through the main proxy group.
tour.trafficBody: Proxy sets the system HTTP/S proxy to Arch. TUN creates a virtual adapter so all apps are routed — on Windows this usually needs the helper service.
tour.serviceBody: For TUN mode, install the service once. If the button is missing, the service is already installed.

errors.chooseProfileOrImport: Choose a profile under Profiles, or import a new one.

import.connectBlurb: Import a subscription or open Profiles to pick one. Name can stay empty - we read Profile-Title when possible.
import.defaultBlurb: Paste your provider link. Leave the name empty to use the server Profile-Title.

home.healthDegradedHint: Connected, but something is not fully healthy (see warnings below). Traffic may still work.
home.healthBrokenHint: Session is in a broken state (often a failed mode switch or reload). Check the message below, try Disconnect, or switch traffic mode.
home.systemProxyTitle: System proxy — HTTP/S via OS settings
home.serviceStoppedHint: IPC service is installed but not running (TUN may be unavailable).

profiles.title: Profiles
profiles.lead: Right-click a profile for extend, merge, rules, and more. Import below or use the shortcut on Home when you have none.

importModal.detectUnknown: Unrecognized — expecting Clash YAML or vless:// / vmess:// / ss:// / trojan:// links
importModal.pastePlaceholder:
Paste a mihomo/Clash config.yaml,
or share links — one per line:
vless://…
vmess://…
trojan://…

editInfo.title: Edit profile info
editInfo.blurb: Display name and subscription link. Leave the URL field empty to keep the current link unchanged.
editInfo.name: Name

fileModal.title: Edit configuration file
fileModal.readErrorLead: Read: {{error}} — you can still paste YAML and save to create the file.

mergeModal.title: Profile merge template
mergeModal.blurbLead: Top-level keys merge into the fetched profile;
mergeModal.blurbTail: for rules, proxy-groups, and provider maps. Applied whenever Arch writes

proxyModal.title: Edit proxy groups
proxyModal.fixYamlBeforeVisual: Fix proxy advanced YAML before switching to Visual mode.
proxyModal.savedBanner: Proxy groups saved into merge template.

rulesModal.title: Edit rules
rulesModal.fixYamlBeforeVisual: Fix rules advanced YAML before switching to Visual mode.

deleteModal.title: Delete profile
deleteModal.bodySuffix: from this device? This does not cancel a remote subscription.

proxies.title: Proxies
proxies.lead: Selectors and url-test groups from the running Arch core. Connect on Home first, then refresh if the list is empty while the subscription loads.
proxies.pingAll: Ping all nodes in this group
proxies.autoGroupHint: This is an auto group (url-test / fallback / load-balance). Mihomo picks the node itself — manual selection is disabled here. Open a select / Manual group to pick a specific node.
proxies.noGroups: No groups yet - wait for the subscription health check, then Refresh.
proxies.connectFirst: Connect on Home to start the embedded core.
proxies.pickGroupHint: Pick a group on the left to see its nodes.

rules.title: Rules
rules.providersEmpty: No rule providers in the current config.
rules.noParsed: Connect on Home first, then press Refresh to load rules.

logs.title: Logs
logs.empty: No log text yet — tap Refresh.
logs.noMatch: No lines match the current filter.
logs.tailOnlyHint: Show only the most recent lines

advanced.lead: Diagnostics and maintenance tools for runtime support.
advanced.connectivityLead: Best-effort probe via webview fetch (no-cors), useful for quick checks.
advanced.deviceIdentityLead: Stable client id and device info—same values sent as HTTP headers on subscription requests. Use when a provider asks for your HWID.
advanced.hwidSendOffNote: x-hwid is not included in subscription HTTP requests while this is off. Other headers (device OS, app version) are still sent so providers can route per-platform.
advanced.runtimeTraceLead: Recent events from the pipeline tracer and runtime supervisor. Copy when filing a bug.
advanced.maintenanceLead: Safe operations to keep runtime healthy without full reset.
advanced.pathsLead: Open key directories in your file manager.
advanced.powerToolsLead: Heavy actions — use when something is stuck.
advanced.toolsLead: Maintenance & recovery — safe-to-heavy actions when something is stuck.
advanced.pathsMovedNote: Runtime dir & config file are under Files & folders below.
advanced.restartCoreHint: Tear down Mihomo and re-spawn it. Reconnects automatically if you were connected.
advanced.resetSubCacheHint: Force a fresh network fetch on the next Refresh.
advanced.reextractBundledHint: Rewrites geoip.dat / geosite.dat from the app bundle into the runtime folder.
advanced.geoNotExtracted: Not extracted yet — Connect once to provision.
